title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
slides: Your Google Docs just auto-suggested something you didn't want.
AI is on by default in tools you already use daily.
Google buried the off switch deep in settings menus.
You can disable it per document or for your whole account.
Check your Tools menu now before it writes your next line.
thread_text:
It's already watching.

Google Docs auto-suggested a phrase you didn't intend—and you only noticed after typing it.

Default creep.

AI is switched on inside Gmail, Calendar, and Drive—whether you asked for it or not.

The hidden toggle.

Google buried the off switch three layers deep in Settings. Most users never find it.

Your choice, sort of.

You can disable AI per document or for your entire account—but only if you know where.

Check this now.

Open any Google Doc and look under Tools. Is AI writing your next sentence without your OK?
video_script: Your Docs are writing for you.
You can kill it per document.
Check Tools before it edits you.

script: Your Docs are writing for you.
You can kill it per document.
Check Tools before it edits you.

instagram.caption:
Your Google Docs just auto-suggested a sentence you didn’t want—and you only caught it after typing.

AI is turned on by default inside Gmail, Calendar, and Drive, whether you asked for it or not. Google buried the off switch three menus deep in Settings.

If you create or publish anything in Docs, you’re letting AI write your next line without your permission.

Open any Google Doc now. Go to Tools. Is that “Help me write” prompt sitting there, waiting for you?
